title: Inbound signature verification does not require (request-target)
description:
In inbox.rs verify_and_fetch_actor (lines 427-435), the code checks that the signature covers 'digest' and 'date', but does NOT require '(request-target)'. Without (request-target) in the signed headers, an attacker who observes or intercepts a valid signed POST to /inbox could replay it to /users/bob/inbox (or vice versa) on the same host, because the signature remains valid — it only covers headers that are identical across both requests.

In practice, most ActivityPub implementations (Mastodon, Pleroma) DO sign (request-target), so the field is usually present. But a malicious server could craft a valid signature omitting (request-target) and deliver the same activity to a different user-specific inbox to trigger duplicate processing.

Impact: activity replay/routing between inbox endpoints on the same host. The signed digest prevents body tampering, but not re-routing.

Fix: require (request-target) in sig_params.headers_list, similar to the existing digest/date checks.
